name: Artemis III
first: First crewed lunar landing since Apollo 17 (1972)
description: The first crewed lunar landing in over half a century. Architecture: NASA's SLS Block 1 launches Orion with 4 crew to a Near-Rectilinear Halo Orbit (NRHO) around the Moon. Pre-positioned at NRHO is a SpaceX Starship Human Landing System (HLS), refilled in low-Earth orbit by tankers before its trans-lunar injection. The Orion crew transfers to HLS; 2 astronauts descend to the lunar south pole region for ~6.5 days of surface ops while 2 remain in NRHO. After surface operations, HLS launches back to NRHO for transfer to Orion and Earth return. Will land the first woman and first person of colour on the Moon.

events.label: SLS LAUNCH
events.note: Orion launches from KSC LC-39B with crew of 4.

events.label: NRHO ARRIVAL
events.note: Orion arrives at Near-Rectilinear Halo Orbit; rendezvous with pre-positioned Starship HLS.

events.label: DESCENT
events.note: 2 crew transfer to HLS; powered descent to lunar south pole.

events.label: ASCENT
events.note: HLS ascent to NRHO after ~6.5 days surface operations.

events.label: SPLASHDOWN
events.note: Orion splashes down in Pacific Ocean.
